name: mTOR inhibition suppressed SASP through IL1A translation control
summary: This mechanistic rapamycin finding anchors an mTOR-to-SASP pathway relevant to senomorphic interpretation.
statement: In senescent-cell models, mTOR activity supported the pro-tumorigenic SASP by promoting IL1A translation, and rapamycin-sensitive mTOR inhibition suppressed SASP output through that pathway.
population_or_model: Senescent human cell models focused on IL1A and SASP signaling
quantitative_note: The source identifies IL1A translation as an mTOR-linked control point for SASP regulation.
caveats: The evidence is mechanistic and largely cell-based.
SASP can have context-dependent tumor-suppressive, immune-surveillance, and repair roles, so suppression is not automatically beneficial.
This finding supports biological plausibility more than clinical readiness.
